seoTitle: Import Bank Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Upload older or offline bank statements into QuickBooks Online using CSV or QBO files when your live feed can't reach them.
intro: When live bank feeds miss historical data or drop sync, manually retyping every transaction wastes hours. This guide shows how to upload a downloaded bank statement file into QuickBooks Online and map it into your books correctly.

steps.heading: Download your statement
steps.body: Log into your bank's portal and download your transaction history as a CSV, QBO, QFX, or OFX file.

steps.heading: Open the upload tool
steps.body: In QuickBooks Online, go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, click the arrow next to Link account, and choose Upload from file.

steps.heading: Upload your file
steps.body: Click Browse file on the drag-and-drop panel and select your downloaded statement.

steps.heading: Map the account and columns
steps.body: Select the matching QuickBooks account, then configure column headers to align date, description, and amount fields correctly.

steps.heading: Import and review
steps.body: Confirm the parsed rows, click Continue and Done, then check your For Review dashboard for the newly uploaded transactions.

faq.question: What file formats can I upload?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online accepts standard files like QBO, QFX, OFX, and CSV depending on what your bank offers.

faq.question: Why do I need to map columns manually for a CSV?
faq.answer: CSV files don't have a standard structure, so QuickBooks needs you to align date, description, and amount columns correctly during import.

faq.question: Can I split spent and received amounts into separate columns?
faq.answer: Yes, the column mapping step lets you configure single or multi-column layouts to align disbursed and received values.

faq.question: Where do imported transactions go after upload?
faq.answer: They land in the For Review queue on your Bank Transactions dashboard, ready to be categorized.
